avoid: Do not promote this ATen reference candidate based on correctness alone.
Do not implement a custom full d x d GEMM for W @ X; cuBLAS should handle the dominant GEMM.
Do not add explicit B.T transpose-copy or other input copies unless correctness absolutely requires it.
Do not switch first to handwritten rank-16 kernels before validating the simpler pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation.
Do not hardcode a single hidden dimension because d is runtime-selected in [3584, 4608].
Do not use result caching as an optimization direction unless repeated identical input tensors are explicitly part of the benchmark contract.
evidence: Correctness passes for all tested dimensions with zero numerical error, so the implementation is functionally valid.
Aggregate speedup is 0.969x, which is slower than the PyTorch reference overall.
Performance is essentially identical at d=3584 and d=4608 but regresses at d=4096 with only 0.918x speedup.
Profile identifies multiple ATen GEMM/add launches, indicating this candidate is still the baseline-style decomposition rather than a lower-overhead cuBLAS implementation.
The main W @ X term is a large d x d SGEMM and should remain a direct cuBLAS SGEMM rather than being replaced by a custom kernel.
There is no evidence this candidate implements the preferred pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM path or accumulates the low-rank term into Y using beta=1.
recommended_actions: Reject this baseline as an optimization candidate because it is correct but not faster overall.
Move to a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation modeled after /workspace/lora/3_sgemm.cu instead of ATen matmul/add composition.
Use direct cuBLAS SGEMM for the main term W @ X.
Avoid explicit B.T materialization; compute the low-rank intermediate directly with cuBLAS.

Use the exact SGEMM mapping: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N with A=X and B=W to produce Y; SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, U shape {d,16}, ldc=d; SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1 to accumulate into Y.
Require or check contiguous CUDA float32 inputs rather than adding input copies in the hot path.
Use at::cuda::CUDAGuard and at::cuda::getCurrentCUDABlasHandle so cuBLAS runs on the correct device and current PyTorch stream.
After validating the pure three-SGEMM path for correctness on all d values, benchmark it against this baseline before considering handwritten rank-16 kernels.
